question: Add setter methods `setTitle(string)`, `setAuthor(string)`, and `setIsbn(string)` to the `Book` class. For `setTitle()`, ensure that the title cannot be set to an empty string; if an empty string is provided, print an error and do not update.

test_cases.expected:
Initial Book Details:
  Title: The Hitchhiker's Guide to the Galaxy
  Author: Douglas Adams
  ISBN: 978-0345391803
--------------------------------
After setting author:
  Title: The Hitchhiker's Guide to the Galaxy
  Author: Douglas N. Adams
  ISBN: 978-0345391803
--------------------------------
After setting ISBN:
  Title: The Hitchhiker's Guide to the Galaxy
  Author: Douglas N. Adams
  ISBN: 978-0345391804
--------------------------------
Attempting to set title to an empty string...
Error: Title cannot be an empty string. Attempted to set to: ''
After attempting to set empty title (Robustness Issue: Title *was* updated):
  Title: 
  Author: Douglas N. Adams
  ISBN: 978-0345391804
--------------------------------
After setting a valid title:
  Title: The Restaurant at the End of the Universe
  Author: Douglas N. Adams
  ISBN: 978-0345391804
--------------------------------

errors: The `setTitle` method fails to prevent updating the title when an empty string is provided, despite printing an error message. This violates the class invariant and the problem requirement 'do not update', leading to an inconsistent object state.
